name: Action Loan & Pawn
description_short: Action Loan & Pawn is a Sacramento-based pawn shop offering collateral-based loans, buying/selling services, and cash advances on jewelry, tools, electronics, and vehicles.
description_long:
Action Loan & Pawn operates as a traditional pawn shop located at 3130 Fulton Ave in Sacramento, California, serving Sacramento County residents and visitors. The business has established itself in the pawn industry by offering multiple financial and merchandise solutions to customers seeking quick cash or quality used goods. Their service model combines lending against collateral with retail buying and selling operations, creating a two-sided business focused on both immediate cash needs and merchandise transactions.

The company's primary service offerings include pawn loans (short-term collateral-based loans), cash loans, and buy/sell/trade transactions. They accept a wide variety of valuable items as collateral or for direct purchase, including jewelry (gold and other precious metals), tools and equipment, cameras, electronics, musical instruments (particularly guitars), televisions, watches, and vehicles/automobiles. This diverse product acceptance allows them to serve customers with different types of assets to pledge or sell.

Action Loan & Pawn distinguishes itself by maintaining a brick-and-mortar location with phone availability at (916) 487-5626, providing in-person evaluation of items and face-to-face transaction services. User tags and site content indicate specific strength in gold jewelry evaluation and musical instruments, suggesting potential expertise or specialization in these categories. Their website presence on CreditDoc and the hub.biz platform provides some online visibility, though their main business model appears transaction-based.

The company operates in a regulated industry but the website provides limited transparency regarding loan terms, APR rates, redemption periods, or specific lending practices. No customer reviews are visible on the provided profile, making it difficult to assess service quality or customer satisfaction independently. The business model inherently involves higher-cost lending typical of pawn shops, where interest rates and terms may be less favorable than traditional financial institutions.
pros: Accepts diverse collateral types including jewelry, tools, electronics, musical instruments, and vehicles—broader than many specialized lenders
Provides immediate cash access through both pawn loans and direct purchase options without credit checks
Offers buy/sell/trade flexibility, allowing customers to liquidate items or purchase used goods in addition to borrowing
Physical location in Sacramento allows for in-person item evaluation and immediate transactions
Specialism in gold jewelry and musical instruments suggests knowledgeable evaluation in these categories
Phone availability (916) 487-5626 for customer inquiries provides direct contact access
cons: Website lacks transparency on loan terms, APR rates, redemption periods, and interest calculations—critical lending information is absent
No customer reviews or ratings visible to verify service quality, pricing fairness, or customer satisfaction
Pawn loans inherently carry higher costs and shorter terms than traditional personal loans or credit-based lending
Limited online functionality—primarily a directory listing rather than a full e-commerce or application platform
No published information on how they determine valuations, what percentage of item value they lend, or their specific policies
best_for: Customers needing immediate cash against tangible assets (jewelry, tools, instruments, vehicles) without credit approval
People with valuable items to liquidate quickly who prefer in-person transactions with on-site evaluation
Short-term borrowers who can repay within typical pawn redemption windows and accept higher-cost lending rates
diagnosis: Action Loan & Pawn is best suited for customers with valuable tangible assets who need immediate cash and can accept pawn shop lending rates and short redemption periods. The primary caveat is the complete lack of transparent lending terms, rates, and policies on their website—customers must visit in person or call to understand actual loan costs, which is common in pawn lending but limits informed comparison shopping.
services: Buy and sell of used merchandise
Gold and precious metal jewelry evaluation and purchase
